G4831.derivation: from a presumed compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3401 (μιμέομαι);

G4833.strongs_def: to render like, i.e. (figuratively) to assimilate

G4856.strongs_def: to be harmonious, i.e. (figuratively) to accord (be suitable, concur) or stipulate (by compact)

G4862.strongs_def: with or together (but much closer than G3326 (μετά) or G3844 (παρά)), i.e. by association, companionship, process, resemblance, possession, instrumentality, addition, etc.

G4894.strongs_def: to see completely; used (like its primary) only in two past tenses, respectively meaning to understand or become aware, and to be conscious or (clandestinely) informed of
G4894.kjv_def: consider, know, be privy, be ware of

G4835.strongs_def: having a fellow-feeling ("sympathetic"), i.e. (by implication) mutually commiserative

G4811.derivation: from a compound of G4810 (σῦκον) and a derivative of G5316 (φαίνω);
G4811.strongs_def: to be a fig-informer (reporter of the law forbidding the exportation of figs from Greece), "sycophant", i.e. (genitive and by extension) to defraud (exact unlawfully, extort)
G4811.kjv_def: accuse falsely, take by false accusation

G4818.strongs_def: to afflict jointly, i.e. (passive) sorrow at (on account of) someone

G4883.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a compound of G719 (ἁρμός) and G3004 (λέγω) (in its original sense of laying);
G4883.strongs_def: to render close-jointed together, i.e. organize compactly

G4859.strongs_def: sounding together (alike), i.e. (figuratively) accordant (neuter as noun, agreement)

G4875.strongs_def: to recruit oneself in company with

G4866.strongs_def: to wrestle in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to seek jointly

G4874.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and a compound of G303 (ἀνά) and G3396 (μίγνυμι);
G4874.strongs_def: to mix up together, i.e. (figurative) associate with

G4841.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and G3958 (πάσχω) (including its alternate);
G4841.strongs_def: to experience pain jointly or of the same kind (specially, persecution; to "sympathize")

G4880.strongs_def: to decease (literally) in company with, or (figuratively), similarly to

G4801.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G2201 (ζεῦγος);
G4801.strongs_def: to yoke together, i.e. (figuratively) conjoin (in marriage)

G4881.strongs_def: to destroy (middle voice or passively, be slain) in company with

G4828.strongs_def: to testify jointly, i.e. corroborate by (concurrent) evidence

G4876.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G473 (ἀντί);
G4876.strongs_def: to meet with; figuratively, to occur

G4815.strongs_def: to clasp, i.e. seize (arrest, capture); specially, to conceive (literally or figuratively); by implication, to aid

G4892.derivation: neuter of a presumed derivative of a compound of G4862 (σύν) and the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος);
G4892.strongs_def: a joint session, i.e. (specially), the Jewish Sanhedrin; by analogy, a subordinate tribunal

G4839.strongs_def: to remain in company, i.e. still live

G4846.strongs_def: to strangle completely, i.e. (literally) to drown, or (figuratively) to crowd

G4827.derivation: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3129 (μανθάνω);

G4847.strongs_def: a native of the same town, i.e. (figuratively) co-religionist (fellow-Christian)

G4822.strongs_def: causative (by reduplication) of the base of G939 (βάσις)); to drive together, i.e. unite (in association or affection), (mentally) to infer, show, teach
G4822.kjv_def: compact, assuredly gather, intrust, knit together, prove

G4844.strongs_def: to partake a beverage in company

G4805.strongs_def: co-yoked, i.e. (figuratively) as noun, a colleague; probably rather as a proper name; Syzygus, a Christian

G4895.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and G1510 (εἰμί) (including its various inflections);
G4895.strongs_def: to be in company with, i.e. present at the time

G4836.strongs_def: to be present together, i.e. to convene; by implication, to appear in aid

G4853.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G5443 (φυλή);
G4853.strongs_def: a co-tribesman, i.e. native of the same country

G4819.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G939 (βάσις);
G4819.strongs_def: to walk (figuratively, transpire) together, i.e. concur (take place)

G4873.strongs_def: to recline in company with (at a meal)
G4873.kjv_def: sit (down, at the table, together) with (at meat)

G4817.strongs_def: to reckon together (with oneself), i.e. deliberate

G4845.strongs_def: to implenish completely, i.e. (of space) to swamp (a boat), or (of time) to accomplish (passive, be complete)

G4829.derivation: middle voice from G4862 (σύν) and G3307 (μερίζω);
G4829.strongs_def: to share jointly, i.e. participate in

G4858.strongs_def: unison of sound ("symphony"), i.e. a concert of instruments (harmonious note)

G4800.strongs_def: to continue to live in common with, i.e. co-survive (literally or figuratively)

G4879.strongs_def: to take off together, i.e. transport with (seduce, passively, yield)

G4823.strongs_def: to give (or take) advice jointly, i.e. recommend, deliberate or determine

G4854.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G5453 (φύω);
G4854.strongs_def: grown along with (connate), i.e. (figuratively) closely united to

G4824.derivation: neuter of a presumed derivative of G4825 (σύμβουλος);
G4824.strongs_def: advisement; specially, a deliberative body, i.e. the provincial assessors or lay-court

G4887.strongs_def: to bind with, i.e. (passively) be a fellow-prisoner (figuratively)

G4816.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and G3004 (λέγω) in its original sense;

G4848.strongs_def: to journey together; by implication, to assemble

G4843.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and a compound of G4012 (περί) and G2983 (λαμβάνω);
G4843.strongs_def: to take by enclosing altogether, i.e. earnestly throw the arms about one

G4807.derivation: of Hebrew origin (H08256) in imitation of G4809 (συκομωραία);

G4852.strongs_def: to say jointly, i.e. assent to

G4826.strongs_def: Symeon (i.e. Shimon), the name of five Israelites

G4897.kjv_def: go in with, go with into

G4863.strongs_def: to lead together, i.e. collect or convene; specially, to entertain (hospitably)
G4863.kjv_def: + accompany, assemble (selves, together), bestow, come together, gather (selves together, up, together), lead into, resort, take in

G4898.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G1553 (ἐκδημέω);
G4898.strongs_def: a co-absentee from home, i.e. fellow-traveller

G4888.strongs_def: to exalt to dignity in company (i.e. similarly) with

G4899.derivation: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G1586 (ἐκλέγομαι);
G4899.strongs_def: chosen in company with, i.e. co-elect (fellow Christian)

G4889.strongs_def: a co-slave, i.e. servitor or ministrant of the same master (human or divine)

G4865.strongs_def: to struggle in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to be a partner (assistant)

G4868.strongs_def: to make up together, i.e. (figuratively) to compute (an account)

G4840.strongs_def: to be at hand together, i.e. now present

G4849.derivation: neuter of a derivative of the alternate of G4844 (συμπίνω);
G4849.strongs_def: a drinking-party ("symposium"), i.e. (by extension) a room of guests

G4864.strongs_def: an assemblage of persons; specially, a Jewish "synagogue" (the meeting or the place); by analogy, a Christian church

G4878.strongs_def: to take hold of opposite together, i.e. co-operate (assist)

G4882.strongs_def: to despatch (on an errand) in company with

G4802.strongs_def: to investigate jointly, i.e. discuss, controvert, cavil

G4886.strongs_def: a joint tie, i.e. ligament, (figuratively) uniting principle, control

G4820.strongs_def: to combine, i.e. (in speaking) to converse, consult, dispute, (mentally) to consider, (by implication) to aid, (personally) to join, attack
G4820.kjv_def: confer, encounter, help, make, meet with, ponder

G4893.derivation: from a prolonged form of G4894 (συνείδω);

G4813.strongs_def: probably akin to G138 (αἱρέομαι); compare G4661 (σκῦλον)); to despoil

G4851.derivation: from G4862 (σύν) and G5342 (φέρω) (including its alternate);
G4851.strongs_def: to bear together (contribute), i.e. (literally) to collect, or (figuratively) to conduce; especially (neuter participle as a noun) advantage
G4851.kjv_def: be better for, bring together, be expedient (for), be good, (be) profit(-able for)

G4891.strongs_def: to rouse (from death) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to revivify (spirtually) in resemblance to

G4812.derivation: from the base of G4813 (συλάω) and (the reduplicated form of) G71 (ἄγω);
G4812.strongs_def: to lead away as booty, i.e. (figuratively) seduce

G4834.strongs_def: to feel "sympathy" with, i.e. (by implication) to commiserate
G4834.kjv_def: have compassion, be touched with a feeling of
